turns.content: What brings you in today?
I've been feeling persistently low for the past several months—no interest in things I used to enjoy, trouble concentrating at work, and I've become unusually irritable with my children.
When did this start?
It began roughly five months ago. There wasn't a single trigger; it just gradually crept in.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?
I experience tension headaches, usually across my forehead and temples, but the primary discomfort is an overall sense of mental heaviness and physical fatigue rather than localized pain.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, I haven't had any fever or chills.
What medications are you currently taking?
I'm not currently taking any medications, including over-the-counter drugs or supplements.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
I have a diagnosis of depression, which has been ongoing for several years.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I have no known allergies to any medications.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?
No, I haven't tried any specific treatments or remedies. I've just been trying to push through it.
Does anything make it better or worse?
Nothing consistently alleviates the symptoms. If anything, work-related stress tends to exacerbate the fatigue and irritability.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's significantly impairing my performance at work—I struggle to maintain focus during complex legal work—and at home, I've become short-tempered with my family, which I find distressing. I also have little appetite and difficulty sleeping through the night.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I don't smoke. I do drink alcohol socially, but not heavily—perhaps a glass of wine or two a couple of times per week.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
To my knowledge, there's no family history of depression or similar mood disorders.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
I've had depressive episodes before, but this one feels more persistent and intrusive than previous ones.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?
Yes, that all makes perfect sense. Thank you for the thorough explanation.
